titleStmt.title: Letter from James D Mitchell to Sir Matthew Nathan, 20 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: James D Mitchell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This letter was sent by James Daniel Mitchell to Matthew Nathan to apply for the position of crown solicitor for King’s County. He describes his relevant experience and states that his father and grandfather both held the position.James Daniel Mitchell (b. 1880) was a solicitor from King’s County (Offaly). Sir Matthew Nathan (1862 – 1939) was the under-secretary for Ireland from 1914 to 1916 when he offered his resignation following the outbreak of the Easter Rising.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary’s Office Registered papers, CSO-RP-1916-5615-10437

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Walcot Parsonstown 20th June 1916 Sir, I beg to apply for the Office of Crown Solicitor
for the Kings County recently vacant by the death of
W. R. F. Barry. I am thirty six years of age and was admitted
a Solicitor in Trinity term 1903. I was apprenticed
to my father, the late Thomas Mitchell who held the
office of Sessional Crown Solicitor for this County
for Twenty two years previous to his death in April
1914. My grandfather held the same appointment in
this County for 25 years. On my fathers death
I applied for the appointment, but the then Attorneyâ
General had to amalgamate the Offices of Crown
and Sessional Crown Solicitor, and W. Barry being
offered the latter Office accepted same. I am quite conversant with the duties attached
to the Office of Crown Solicitor having done
deputy for my father and the late W. Barry on
several occasions. I had charge of Crown cases
both at Quarter Sessions and Assizes and
have attended Revision Sessions both
here and in Tullamore. I am local
Solicitor to the Irish Land Commission,
and collect all the Land Purchase Arrears
for the Kings County. I have applied to the Attorney General
for the appointment. I remain
Sir,
Your obedient Servant
Jas D. Mitchell To
The Under Secretary
Dublin Castle
